actions.detail: role 'Classification Analyst' bound to permission group
role 'Screening Analyst' bound to permission group
role 'Licensing Specialist' bound to permission group
role 'Trade Compliance Manager' bound to permission group
role 'Trade Audit Lead' bound to permission group

deferred.reason: Trade-domain policies (no release while a plausible denied-party match is unresolved; a sanctions hold can only be lifted with manager approval and a documented distinguishing rationale; corrected entries and prior disclosures never filed without human sign-off; screening thresholds cannot be tuned down without an adjudication-history review) require guardrail-service / OPA policy materialization, which Core does not expose as a pack surface yet. The behavioral core is partially covered today by Core invariants: every pack-configured agent is proposal-mode with four-eyes approval (agent-runtime), and the agent instructions + disposition taxonomy route holds, releases, and corrected entries exclusively to human analysts with the Trade Compliance Manager as approver.

Bespoke LangGraph recipes (Classification Copilot with GRI-ordered heading analysis, Screening Match Adjudicator, License Determination Assistant, Origin Verification Sentinel) require agent-runtime graph-module registration (BRD 23 BR-12). This pack specializes the existing case-triage + analytics agents via TenantAgentConfig prompt_params + tenant-scope RAG grounding instead.

Production read connectors (broker/ABI entry feeds and ACE reports; TMS/forwarding systems such as CargoWise-class platforms; ERP purchase and supplier masters; denied-party screening vendors' consolidated list feeds; carrier manifests) require customer credentials and pack-service connector materialization. The pack ships seed datasets in the exact landing shape; production connectors are configured through the product Data > Connections UI.

Write adapters (file post-summary corrections / corrected entries via the broker, transmit hold/release instructions to the forwarder or broker system, draft prior-disclosure and voluntary self-disclosure packages for CBP/OFAC/BIS, submit license applications) execute only behind pack-service's materialization contract and customer credentials; every such write stays proposal-mode with human approval — no autonomous holds, releases, filings, or disclosures, ever.

Golden eval sets (HS classification review, screening match adjudication, origin/transshipment risk ranking, license-determination escalation) require eval-service write APIs whose action names are not yet in the rbac canonical catalog (Core follow-up noted in rbac catalog.go).

Ontology registration (Shipment, EntryLine, HsCode, OriginClaim, TradingPartner, ScreeningAlert, WatchListEntry, LicenseDetermination, DutyExposure, PriorDisclosure, DeadlineClock) awaits pack-service; entity semantics are carried today by the trade_core semantic model and dataset schemas.

Typed case schemas (hs_classification_review, screening_adjudication, origin_verification, license_determination, prior_disclosure_review) await case-service schema materialization; today's cases carry the same fields via display_projection + the disposition taxonomy.

Model archetypes (classification_confidence, screening_match_risk) await experiment-service archetype APIs; the scoring capability ships as pipeline seeds instead (isolation_forest shipment anomaly + xgboost review-outcome scorer) with model-governance expectations noted in grounding memories.

Display-label overlays ("Review item", "Screening alert", "Corrected entry due", "Hold pending adjudication", entity templates) require the BFF label-registry endpoints (BFF-FR-082/088) which are pack-service-tier features.
